exemplar.title: Does some knowledge belong only to particular communities of knowers?

detailedExemplarData.title: Does some knowledge belong only to particular communities of knowers?

strengths: Three specific objects are clearly identified with precise real-world contexts (RTX 3090 Ti release details; Channel Oneâs state control; Enigmaâs WWII use).
Consistent, coherent links are made between each object and the IA prompt about knowledge belonging to particular communities.
Justifications for inclusion are generally persuasive and grounded in evidence, demonstrating understanding of how each object contributes to the overarching TOK theme.
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally drifts into descriptive or assertive claims without sufficient theoretical framing in TOK terminology.
Evidential support is unevenâcitations are sparse, improperly formatted, and lack depth or diversity of sources.
Connections to broader TOK concepts (e.g. authority, knowledge production, power dynamics) could be more explicitly and systematically integrated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although strand assessment places the work in band 5â6 (6/10) due to uneven evidential support and occasional over-description, the official examinerâs total mark of 10 is final. There is clear, specific contextualization of three objects, coherent prompt linkage, and solid justification throughout. Under reverse grading, we allocate the full 10 marks to the single TOK Exhibition criterion to reflect the examinerâs determination.

annotations.comment: The image of the 3090-Ti is appropriate, but the student should integrate specific visual features (e.g., CUDA core layout) into their commentary to deepen contextual understanding.
Clear and precise caption identifying the specific real-world object demonstrates strong contextualization, satisfying the requirement for specificity.
The description of CUDA cores and DLSS is detailed but dominates the commentary; condense technical background to sharpen focus on knowledge restriction implications.
The claim that patent expiration will âfuture-proofâ Nvidiaâs lead oversimplifies market dynamics; discuss how competitors might innovate post-patent to add nuance.
Only one citation (Nvidia, 2022) is provided and lacks a URL or page reference; strengthen the evidential base with more precise and varied sources.
Link the legal mechanism of patents explicitly to TOK concepts such as âknowledge productionâ and âauthority,â defining these terms to enhance theoretical rigor.
The assertion that consumers âmust get it from Nvidiaâ neglects alternative open-source developments; consider how community-driven solutions challenge this exclusivity.
The identification of CORâs launch date and government control provides a well-established context, demonstrating clear real-world grounding.
The broadcast image is relevant, but annotate specific elements (logo, presenters) to illustrate how visual cues reflect state influence.
Vivid descriptions of propaganda lack in-text citations; ensure references like Anonymous (2023) are clearly linked to specific claims.
Claiming severe consequences for unauthorized broadcasts requires concrete examples or cases; add specific incidents to substantiate the argument.
The oversimplification of media ownership ignores private broadcasters; acknowledge exceptions or comparative cases to show depth.

To maintain a clear link with the IA prompt, the student should explicitly restate the research question within the analysis of each object, reinforcing the connection to âknowledge belonging to particular communities.â
How might comparisons with other state-run channels (e.g., BBC, CCTV) reveal whether knowledge restriction is a universal practice?
Strengthen theoretical grounding by linking state media control to TOK ideas of âpower dynamicsâ and âgatekeepingâ in knowledge dissemination.
Precise mention of the Enigma inventor and era shows strong contextualization, fulfilling the requirement for specificity.
Include an annotated diagram of the rotor mechanism to illustrate why cryptographic expertise remained confined to a small community.
The statement that âonly a select hundred ever had accessâ requires archival or secondary-source evidence; provide specific historical data.
Clarify what constituted âexpertiseâ in WW II engineering and how it was recognized, to support the claim about exclusive knowledge holders.
Link the cracking of Enigma to TOK concepts such as âshared knowledgeâ and the shift of communities of knowers during the collaboration at Bletchley Park.
The claim about capturing 300 Enigmas needs a clear citation and context; specify the source and elaborate on how this quantity aided knowledge dissemination.
Could analysis of British and Polish contributions to Enigma decryption offer a counterpoint to the claim that knowledge belonged solely to German experts?
The reference to âAnonymous. âHow All of Russian TV Became State-Controlledââ lacks publisher information and proper format; revise to meet IB guidelines.
Web citations require access dates and consistent style; add retrieval dates for URLs and unify the format across references.
The citation for Imperial War Museums should list the organization as author, medium, and retrieval date to enhance credibility and completeness.
Include direct URLs and retrieval dates for the Nvidia developer resources to improve traceability and strengthen academic rigor.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?

three objects, but the

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
